reviews.review_text:
stfu i hate my life

mind you, THIS IS GONNA BE MY ONE AND ONLY REVIEW.

i just finished this and i genuinely don’t think i’ve ever cried this hard over a game before.
this is the ONLY game where i would actually erase my entire memory just to experience it for the first time again

yeah, i could do a second playthrough but it will NEVER hit the same. ever.

the soundtrack is genuinely perfect. every song fits the atmosphere so well that i honestly can’t imagine the game without it. the music IS the soul of this game. huge love to shihoko hirata because wow

and the characters… i don’t even know how to explain it properly. they felt so real and human that i got way too emotionally attached to all of them. every interaction felt meaningful, every bond felt genuine, and by the end it felt like i was saying goodbye to actual friends.

that’s why this game destroyed me emotionally. it wasn’t just sad.. it felt real

i don’t think persona 4 golden is just a game honestly. it’s an experience i’ll probably carry with me forever ;(

This is in my opinion the best out of the main three modern Persona games, it mixes all the aspects I love from the other two games to make truly one of the best video games I've ever played. The story is great, the characters are spectacular, the music is spectacular, the graphics are cool, and the gameplay is pretty cool too. Please play this game if you like Persona or JRPG's in general.

By the way, I played them in the order of Persona 5 Royal, Persona 4 Golden, and then Persona 3 Reload within like a year.

This is the most addictive game I ever played, the gameplay, story, music and characters make a incredible experience. My only problem is the beginning, it feels kinda slow. Other than that play this game as soon as you can.

10/10 will play again.

Easily hands down the best game I've ever played. It's not even close. This title is a bit dated compared to Royal and Reload but if you're willing to play a bit of an older game, this is the best in the series. Almost every character is beautifully written with only a few seldom outliers being some minor social links that aren't important to the main story. Combat is intuitive and flows nicely, as it usually does in ATLUS titles. Persona fusions are great (with the exception of Kaguya) and you can really make some broken stuff. 

Overall this is probably the only game I'd give a wholehearted 10/10 score on all fronts. The music is the best in the series, the combat is classic, and the story is wonderful no matter how many times you play through it.
If you want to play a game that makes you happy play this. If you want to play a game that makes you sad play this. If you want to play any game in general please just play persona 4 golden.
best game ever btw, great story, great characters, great soundtrack. the PERFECT game to me. sure it has its flaws in gameplay (atleast compared to 5) but i still prefer it over any persona game

Persona 4 was there when i felt my loneliest in my life, Being apart of a group of friends in the game and genuinely having fun in the ingame dungeons really made my days brighter and made a false sense of nostalgia for 2000s (even though the game is set in 2011) japan. 

With the remake fully being shown off at the xbox games reveal stream im ready to re-enter the world of persona. I still highly recommend golden as it embodies the nostalgic feeling well.
I reach out 2 the truth of dis game, and I found that it wuz peak, god bless Atlus that it finally gets a remake.

reach out to the truth. 

everything about this game is golden. the scooby doo mystery, charming small town vibe, heavenly music, fun gameplay, hilarious anime cutscenes, and my favorite cast of characters make this game truly stand out as a work of art.

easy 10/10

Persona 4: Golden is a game I love to go back to over and over again. After realizing P4 Revival is around the corner, I thought I would revisit Golden again for the 4th time now, and its still a great time. 
The game is filled with great characters, amazing music, a snappy turned based fighting style, and a great story filled with mystery. No matter how many times I play this game, I'm always invested in the story and the characters surrounding it. 
If you're familiar with P3 or P5, you're going to feel at home with this P4G. Navigate a rural town in Japan, go to school, make friends, and save the world. You will have to choose what you do strategically since you're on a in game calendar time crunch. The game is 80% vitural novel/20% dungeon crawler, so expect a lot of reading with some great voice acting, that being said...
My criticism of the game lies on something that just cant be helped, due to Golden being a re-release of the original game, they added more story content, which means they needed to do some new voice acting. Sadly, for me, the new voice acting is not up to par and its a little jarring when the voice acting jumps from the original to the new and back to the original. It's not the biggest deal, but it's something I will always notice. To put it in perspective, if the original VC was a 10, the new VC added gets an 8/10. Not the biggest offence, but worth mentioning. 
The replay ability of this game is immense. As I stated, I've played through this game 4 times now, and I'm doing new things that I haven't done in the past. Even after playing it, I'm tempted to do a 5th run with NG+, something i haven't done before. 
 P4G is a game I recommend to anyone who is interested in JRPG's, mystery novels, and slice of life anime. This game is a classic and can't be missed.

The best persona game in the series in my opinion, almost flawless, i had to get this game again as there are very nice and wholesome moments in the game making it feel like your second home. i love this game a lot as you get really attached to the cast and setting of the game.
The Scooby-Doo gang of the person games. Amazing story and love the characters. As always the gameplay is one of my favorites

This game is definitely my favorite game ever. From the story, to the gameplay, to the soundtrack, I just love everything about this game.

Everyday is great at your Junes!!!
absolutely love this game, the cast is so fun and it really feels like you're part of the investigation team, the music goes HARD but the combat can be a pretty boring marathon

PEAK PERSONA
Persona 4 Golden genuinely surprised me and shattered any expectations I had for it. 
I am so grateful to have experienced this journey around seeking the truth and accepting your true self in the end. This game has one of the best cast of characters I've seen in a game that feel like genuine friends and family that you feel sad to leave behind. This to me stands as ATLUS's magnum opus.
10/10 Also banger music like always

So far I've only played this Persona game, but I can say for sure this will be my favorite. The characters in this game are amazing and they actually feel like real friends. Honestly shed some tears at the end, I 100% recommend this game with all my heart if you're looking for a great JRPG with an amazing cast and interactions with them!

Certainly (just a little) dated if you're used to the newer games, but it has it's charm. Storywise is also just as good as the other two modern games in the series. I would recommend playing this version even if you were for whatever reason just looking forward to P4R. I will say though, due to the games themes, the good endings can be a little convoluted to reach. Be diligent in where you save, though the game will warn you sometimes.

Hantal ve yorucu bir oyun. Zamanına göre 2008'e göre belki eğlenceli olabilir. Alıcaksanız revival'ı bekleyin. Müzikleri 10/10, hikayesi orta üstü diyebilirim herhalde. En can sıkıcı tarafı çok olan arkadaşlık kardeşlik kısımları. Gerçi oyun zaten arkadaşlığın etrafında dönüyor, başından beri öyle olacağını biliyorsun da.. Fena oyun değil ama eğlene eğlene bitireceğinizi sanmayın. Özellikle level atlamak için grind yapmanız lazım olacak ve sıkkınlıktan bayılacaksınız. Oyunu kolayda oynamanızı öneririm. Çok bir anlamı yok zorluğun bu oyunda bence.

Mal gibi farketmeden normal ending aldıktan sonra save'in üstüne ng+'ı kaydettim. True ending almadan dönüyorum. Lanet olsun.

My first JRPG, and it's unlike any other game I've played.
The setting is colorful, the characters unique and there's just something cozy about the game (even though it does have it's stressful moments). I loved that almost every line is voiced by an all-star cast of voice actors. Easily one of the best games I've played. 
I'm picking up Persona 5 after this and looking forward to playing more Atlus games.

Really felt like I'd grown and matured together with the main cast by the end. I started off not really being into this game, but the more I kept playing the more I fell in love with it and the entire crew's dynamic. They all feel like a big family.

I know the remake is coming out soon, but I recommend doing yourself a favour and playing this despite the lack of some QoL from more modern titles like P3R which I came from.

I started off not really liking the older look of the game, but as I played more and more I not only got used to it but it added to my experience. It gives the game a sort of warm feeling that made it all the more cozy and enjoyable for me. I finished this game right before my summer vacation from college, yet the entire thing FELT like a summer vacation. OST is peak as well ofc

I'm sad that it's over and I'm never gonna forget this game. Sure its got its flaws, but to me it was a masterpiece.


"There's a lot we'd rather not see... A lot of grime in the human heart.
But if you close your eyes to everything, you can't even see the people close to you.
How can you be happy like that?
The only thing to do, really, is to keep marching on, straight ahead." -Ryotaro Dojima
Good game so far, really chill to enjoy on the Steam deck in bed. Some days just pass too fast imo, especially when all i wanna do is go to the velvet room but another char wanna do something else 😅

In the beginning, I had only thought that P4G was too old of a game to even play given I started only 5 hours back in February 2025. I picked it up again to give it another fair shot and this time with an open mind. The investigation team is by far my favourite cast of characters as they emanate so much personality, joking back and forth with each other. The soundtrack is as always phenomenal, shuffle time is still an addicting mechanic to keep you in the gameplay loop and the themes of identity and truth resonate with me profoundly. I cannot wait until Persona 4: Revival gets released and I can only recommend playing this game first to better judge how much better P4R will be. P4G is a game with a lot of heart and is a game I will remember for many years to come.

Мне кажется про эту игру лучше всего сказать "лучше один раз увидеть, чем сто раз услышать". 

  Сюжет и персонажи трогают до глубины души, настолько, что аж могут пробить на эмоции. Да, не без минусов конечно, но история все равно очень даже стоит того. 
  Графика чисто пс2, только немного подтянуто разрешение и спрайты. Сильно пробивает на ностальгию игр тех лет. 
  Геймплей я лично считаю очень приятным и разнообразным. В ТВ-мире можно крафтить персоны, выбирать скиллы для них, находить карты со скиллами и потом прививать их отдельным персонам. Да, можно задушится в некоторых данжах, но это всё-таки жрпг, и тут даже не настолько все плохо, как в той же персоне 3. 
  Атмосфера тут очень густая, прямо как туман (намеренный каламбур). Всё это жизнеутверждающее настроение и музыка солнечных дней, молчаливая пустота дождливых дней, уютные, но в то же время меланхоличные зимние пейзажи погружают в игру как можно сильно и не отпускают до самого конца. То же можно сказать и про ТВ-мир, который живет собственной жизнью. Хаотичный, где-то мрачный, где-то светлый, но всё же чарующий своей неопределенностью.
  Музыка... ну это персона, вы должны понимать, что об аудио-составляющей тут можно не беспокоиться.

  Отдельно хотелось бы рассказать про посыл игры, но очень вкратце и без спойлеров (я постараюсь). 
Игра затрагивает множество тем, которые будут актуальны всегда: одиночество, ложь, самообман, конформизм, поиск самого себя, стремление к истине. История настолько хорошо вплетает эти темы в нарратив, и решает их столь же хорошо, что пройденный путь запоминается надолго, а ее темы становятся пищей для размышления о своей собственной жизни. Я ставлю игре 9.5/10 и очень ее советую всем небезразличным к проектам подобного жанра. 
  "Pursue your true self".

Persona 4 Golden is a great experience for JRPG fans and is the perfect entry point for trying the series. Runs great on Steam Deck. Low cost to pickup. Engaging story that will intrigue you from the start and keep you playing through its 50+ hour runtime. 

The small town setting of this game mirrors the plot when compared to Persona 3 and 5: not as large, or grandiose of a conflict as those games, but still having stakes that you become invested in and feel more personal. The cast of party members will very quickly grow on you. The Investigation Team as a whole feels like the closest friend group out of the series and Persona 4 is definitely the most high-school feeling game as well.

Plenty of difficulty modifiers make this game very easy to pick up and breeze through the challenge if you're more interested in the story, or you can crank up the difficulty if you're seeking out a challenge.

I don't think this game hits as high for me as Persona 3 or 5 -- mainly due to the plot, music, and gameplay. However a remake of this game is on the horizon so a lot of those aging game mechanics may be resolved in Persona 4 Revival whenever that drops. The music is still incredibly catchy and the gameplay is not that different from the rest of the series. But a new coat of paint, and an art style that has more realistic character models compared to the Chibi ones in this game (which was original a PS VITA title) should do wonders for the remake. This game is still a fantastic time and everyone that loves this series will have a different one as their personal favorite.

*Minor Spoiler Warning*

Largest issue with the game is that there are multiple endings, one of them being the True ending, and a significant amount of content that comes with that. It is incredibly cryptic on how to get the True Ending and I cannot imagine how one would figure it out without a guide. Some elements of it may seem obvious, but the amount of dialogue choices that you need to make (not just once but multiple separate instances) can have you skipping a ton of content without realizing it. Highly recommend a guide to avoid missing out. The other bummer is that the extra content narratively is great but gameplay wise is rough. You will either be on the same loop of doing social links for way too long or going through the worst dungeon in the game. The narrative and true ending far outweigh the negatives, but the pacing feels rough. Especially compared to Persona 5 Royal where the extra content was paced perfectly.
Bring back so many memories here, personally the best persona game. GOAT

In short: The GAMe that is Persona 4 Golden, absolutely BLOWS

In all fairness I put this game on the hardest difficulty due to ego reasons HOWEVER the combat and my experience with the game was so grindy and overall the dungeon crawling in itself was not enjoyable YET to be fair, im sure the game is more reasonable on a regular difficulty + you can just play this game on story mode and not need to dungeon crawl in the first place

Yet now you're probably wondering: "if this game blows, why are you recommending it?" Well the answer is simple: although the gameplay, from my experience, absolutely sucked, everything else about the game was PHENOMENAL. 

The main cast of characters felt extremely real and important and were all extremely well developed with each main character feeling just as important as the other. Not only that, the social links were all in all extremely good and in my personal opinion I really liked the pretext of "facing your other self" as the basis for most if not all the confidants (Rise's confidant was my favorite by far)

Oh and to top it all off? The music was just extremely good. Nothing more to say. Snowflakes is my favorite song from the game.

Oh yea and I guess the story is a plus aswell, though the "twist" is sadly pretty easily recognizable + the "Golden" content is virtually non-existent on doesn't add much if any value to the story in itself the game could have honestly ended right after the last boss, and for narrative reasons it just makes alot more sense that it would.

Yet despite all this the gameplay still sucked.

This game is amazing, yet its even better if you dont "play" it. Turn on storymode and this may be a legitimate 10/10
初めてやったペルソナシリーズのゲームだけど、ストーリーめっちゃオモロイと思うんよ

Persona 4 Golden was absolutely amazing from beginning to end. 
From the story, soundtracks, social links, and dungeons. Everything was put together very well and created a addicting and impactful story game. 
The main cast from this persona were perfect. I found myself immersed in the game, they make you feel like you were truly part of this group of friends. 
This game sets the bar high and was able to make me experience a wide range of emotions. It is a game where anyone can find comfort in.  
This game will rank very high for me and is definitely worth trying/playing once more.

When compared to Persona 5 (my first Persona game), they never should have done away with Shuffle Time. Either I missed something major in P5, or they removed a lot of ability to fine tune personas like in 4.
-I enjoy the skill card shop in this far more than in P5.
-The main character cast feels a lot more authentic (VAs did phenomenal)
-Banger Soundtrack (P5's was also quite good)
However, the story takes a nose dive in quality around the last third. I personally think P5's was worse overall, but that last bit was still a bit disappointing.

Overall just a fun game.
good game, ok graphics, good soundtracks

This game is so fun so far I love the characters and the interactions they have with each other it really makes me feel happy. The characters' struggles feel very real and are very expanded upon allowing them to have so much more about them. I also like the setting of the game and the overall story so far. I do not like the gameplay loop of dungeons as they feel very mundane and slow, but this isn't much of a problem because the combat itself itself is pretty fun but quite challenging. The overall feel of this game is so spectacular.

For me, P4G is the greatest of all time !

私にとって、P4Gが一番最高のゲームです！

對我來說 P4G 才是天下第一 !!

So nostalgic!

I first played the regular (not golden) Persona 4 on the PS2, which was still in Japanese, around 2007-2009 (I don't remember much) and managed to beat it using a guide in a gaming magazine.

Now I'm playing the 2025 & 2026 Golden versions again, with Japanese dubbing and subtitles, and lots of new features. It's really fun.
Masterpiece parah sih, apalagi versi Golden yg juga punya beberapa tambahan sentuhan. I hope it still same in Revival soon

This game is amazing and i love all of the characters.

The music is so perfect too

I love these games
its fine just make sure to add the community mods that add to the experience

Played this after Persona 5 Royal, the older graphics are quite charming and the group dynamic is great! I liked the main cast a lot and gameplay, even though it was a little more dated was still a lot of fun
Thank you floor 5 of naoto's dungeon, i wouldn't be where i am without you. Best game in best franchise ever, life changing.
one of the best games i have played. Characters are fun and well written a shame it has to end
Amazing game except if you're James and you are fighting the dice and the king from Yukiko's dungeon
the only game to ever have me happy angry and tear up. By far the greatest game ive ever played

I don't care if you were born in 1968 or 2008, get this game. Let it into your bloodstream. Run up the roof or whatever building you're in and scream out "PERSONA!!!!!" at the top of your lungs.

It's more than an JRPG. It's a psychological treasure of memorable characters, great times, and unique experiences. This is not at all the type of game to speedrun through, so newcomers really make sure you're taking your time with this game--it's very beautiful from start to finish.

Overall, it's a decent game. 
I do not like the social aspects of the game, but they are done well enough if you like that kind of thing.
I do like the mystery aspect and the characters, while none really stood out to me, were decent.
The Persona series is a series that I want to love, but I can't get around the social and schedule mechanics.

I love this game alot, but if you want to 100% the game, GET A GUIDE, don't spend another 90-100 hours trying to figure it out on your own, mainly with the stupid fox social link that ruined my 40 hour run because it had a deadline.
Great story, characters. Gameplay is ok in dungeons but great atmosphere espec. with banger music. Revival is gonna be the best jrpg ever for sure

I got this game because I thought P5 looked real stylish but I didn't have a console (it wasn't on PC yet). I don't finish RPGs. I finished this one. It had me playing until I saw the sunrise every night for a week.

Persona 4 is at the very least a rather unique game, whereas all other persona games in the series (Including P1 and P2) take place in big cities this one takes place in a small town and the stakes seem significantly less important at first. Whereas others have themes like every conspiracy theory becoming real at the same time or gods trying to manipulate mankind here you’re just presented with the problem of solving a murder mystery in the aforementioned small town, you get to meet and become friends with just about everyone in town including the killer themselves and the time limits make sense as that’s the time limit before the person the killer is after is killed.

Gameplay-wise I’m not that big of a fan, it carries the stupid card system to get new monsters and the same generic monsters in rogue-like dungeons from Persona 3. If you haven’t played either the gist of it is that at the end of every battle you get three cards that spin like a roulette machine that you can stop to get what’s on them [b]sometimes[/b] one of the things you can get is a monster but most of the time it’s not and you can lose a lot of time if you’re after collecting a specific monster in a dungeon and chances are, [i]you are[/i], because the game constantly requires you to fuse monsters in order to make better ones and most of the time you’ll be missing one monster with a 5% chance to spawn at the end of the battle, the battles being against generic beings like hands or buff black silhouettes wearing masks, they get rather old.

I’m also not huge on the music of this one? It’s not bad but I don’t think it’s anywhere near as memorable as the soundtrack of P3 or P5.

The area where the game exceeds at in my opinion are the characters, honestly this game may have one of the most likable cast of characters out there, you may dislike some and that’s okay but honestly I’d find it hard to believe if you dislike all of them. The dungeons are a boring PITA but living in the small town getting to meet all the inhabitants of it before the timer runs out on you is really what I could consider the main attraction of the game.
What an Absolutely beutiful journey. even if you want to wait for revival or straigh up play golden. please. experience this game story
Honestly the coziest(?) one in the series and it has Kanji with Naoto, so it's automatically the best.

This is my favorite game of all time and probably forever because it made me get out of my shell in junior year of highschool and try and make more friends in my senior year so it will always have a special place in my heart.

I just started this gamesbt 5 hours ago, but I already ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ love it. the ONLY thing is the dungeons and saving are uhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhh blegh. butmost of those problems are bcus its an older Persona game, but the rmke will prob fix that (I still wanna finish this first tho)

what i like
- I like the murder mystery vibe and the overall concept of tv (not gonna explain since its a spoiler). 
- the dungeons having aesthetics/concepts tied to the characters' personality and trauma is a really neat detail. 
- art style and ost = god tier
- characters are likeable and you wont regret building social links with them since their stories are actually interesting (idk what to say about the fox tho)
- This game got Kanji so there's that
- the main group feels like actual friend group. theyre there not for the sake of giving the protagonist some sort of company. it feels natural and organic
- 2nd playthrough will actually make you feel dumb (if you didnt pay that much attention) with how much details you gonna miss out while playing. (personally i think thats a good thing)
- this game got Nanako so thats another + points
- Chie exist


questionable stuff
-probably skill issue but the shadow hitbox
-Teddie bear puns
-Ayane's hair
-Grim reaper being in a chest/box
-no option to slap king Moron
-no option to slime king Moron yourself
-no option to slime those partimer bihs at junes
-Margaret kissing scene
-no option to grab/touch Igor's nose
-no option to slime those bullies (during Chie's social link story)
- no option to slime Ai
- no option to slime Kashiwagi
- no option to slime Hanako
- no option to use Chie and Yukiko's curry as consumable that inflicts poison to enemies
- no option to use Rise's spicy omelette as consumable that deals severe almighty damage to enemies
- no option to use Nanako's chocolate as consumable that deals severe almighty damage to enemies with a 100% chance of inflicting poison
- no option to slime that fish eyes
- no butler set for girls
- no maid set for boys



Every day's great at your Junes
I would give anything for my memory of this game to be wiped so I could play it for the first time again. 10/10.
Buen juego, buen final, buenas viejas, buenota la Rise. Excelente juego

TL;DR: Although several aspects of it have aged, the core of what makes P4G good is still there: a great story with several twists, deep character backgrounds, and of course, insanely good music.

Coming from either remake of P3 or P5 this one will initially feel like a downgrade: the models are visibly aged, a lot of combat conveniences are not there, and the dungeon crawling is way too grindy for my taste, at least without increasing the XP gain modifier. There is a general silliness of course, and frankly Teddie's and Rise's voices are annoying for different reasons (at least in the Japanese VO), but this is a story about high-schoolers after all. But the world eventually manages to suck you in, the characters and story are well written.

Despite some parts feeling a bit too teen-oriented and fanservicey, the story deals with some mature topics in a surprisingly thoughtful way, bringing its metaphors to life quite literally and leaving you with a satisfying conclusion if you invest in it.

Persona 4 Golden takes you to a quiet town, Inaba, a countryside civilization with breath of fresh air, clean lake, and festive community that need to support each other in order to survive from external economy competition franchise like Junes, a big department store. A quiet countryside town hit close to home experience that Word-of-Mouth like Urban Legends are still a strong influence to put people be wary of environment. Be that as may, a cheerful friendship, haunting mystery, and a big responsibility awaits you, the Hero. I have to say, it was a good experience.

I really love the Social Links experience. There's so many good characters with good stories within them. As the game mechanic goes, the Social Links have their own proper role too as an Arcana. Sayoko, the Devil Arcana, she has a mischiveous intent on an introduction, but as you get to know her, she has a genuine intention despite being seen as "Devil" by others whom doesn't know her. I admire her so much because as Devil, her story is as good as from other Persona game like Tanaka or better than Ohya. A faithfulness and the authencity of the role of Arcana is what makes her a good side-character. Other example of a good character is Rise, a Lovers Arcana. The role of Lovers usually put the team together with nurture and care. A lot of characters in the game play their role properly and not being too much overhauled by others. But with how good the character developments are, some characters in the end played their role too much and ended up becoming a bad character.

There's so many characters that has good character development and it's not just from the Social Links hangouts, but the way it told from story, battle, cutscenes, or slice-of-life gigs. I can't explain them all, but characters like Yukiko, Rise, Kanji, Marie, Sayoko, Kou, Dojima,or Naoto played their role perfectly. Not only they have good stories, but their dialogue and performance are really good. Who knows, Yukiko that is portrayed as composed, elegant, and firm female, is really cheerful and funny around friends, a side she could never show to strangers. That trait is developed through time, where she was really careful and quiet, but then becoming her real self. That's just one of the best of the best, but some other character is written really badly, everytime he talks, I rolled my eyes. What is up with Teddie? 

This is might be the lows-of-the lows in Persona 4 Golden. The game doesn't know when to stop when the gags are done. I really love Teddie, until he took a form, becoming a total pervo and weirdo, EVEN AFTER he became a better person. His story was so good, but his writing made everything ten times worse. I cringed so hard, a fun school festival, that can be shown wholesome-ly, turned into a creep-fest, where Teddie wants the girl to wear a SWIMSUIT? I laughed myself off because how funny the first part was, a harmless cross-dressing that is playful and fun, and then it took a weird turn. What I love with Persona games is that it can be grounded, nostalgic, and fun, but it's too comical and just, bad. There's so many other slice-of-life gigs that drag too much and took a weird turn because this guy is included in everything. There is an homage for Persona 3 in this game that I look forward to, not until it became drunk-fest cringe because Teddie wants to understand the human world, like what is wrong with this writing? Not only he's the worst character, his character development doesn't go anywhere, unlike Morgana. There's so many fillers dialogue that makes the gigs became awkward because the game doesn't know what to do with their character like Chie, Yosuke, and Teddie. Chie and Yosuke doesn't really have any proper development because they're used as tutorial character, Chie social links doesn't go anywhere and the only thing she can talk is instead of her story is other character like Yukiko. And the thing is, I really love the slice-of-life gigs because the wholesome friendship.

I really love the team friendship in this game. Some character are not scared to approach other and took an interest like Kanji to Naoto, Yosuke to Chie, and Rise to protagonist despite I chose Yukiko, and not being platonic. To be fair, the only reason they didn't commit on their relationship is to make main character have more options on romance, but I can even clearly see who their interest is. Not only that, the wholesome friendship, brainstorming dialogue, and drama are fairly balanced and not being overhauled by other character. This is like the best friendship Persona game have. Unlike Persona 5, Makoto, Morgana and Futaba take over most of the dialogue, making them mostly talk and just letting Ryuji, Yusuke and Ann saying filler dialogue, the rest is balanced, granted. It's not just that, but the theme of the game is just really cheerful and fun, despite being a underclass student, Kanji, Rise, and Naoto are not scared to interact with Chie, Yosuke, and Yukiko and treated them as friends. It's the most grounded experience.

The romances are really fun too. Yukiko, the love of my life, having the best character development through main story and hangouts. So many slice-of-life gigs like Skiing, Christmas, Valentines, Shrine Festival, and new years that giving so many opportunity for your romance options to hangout with you. Not only that, you can flirt with them during main-story dialogue, that is if your lover is on the team. And I really love females with good writing too, therefore I chose Yukiko. Her romance dialogue are really cute and flirty, in par with other Priestess like Makoto and Fuuka, but still, I love Yukiko more.

The premise of urban legend is the most interesting one to me. Despite having a cheerful, entertaining, and colorful theme, the game can be haunting if we're paying attention to the mystery of a countryside town. It's really realistic, because people can brush it off when it comes to horror story, but when the local says, "You have to be respectful to the local folklore", as someone who's a city-boy and a stranger in a new town, the protagonist need to take it seriously. The idea of midnight channel, watching it on rainy evening, to know who's the next victim is, with daunting piano, it's sooo spooky. And this premise alone is so fun. The story overall is really good too, I love it. And as a concept of Television, to show the audience the side of victims they never know, is really interesting. It can be repetitive at times because two times we're rescuing someone to face their other-self can be fun, but ten times?! Now you're just pulling my pizzle here. It's still entertaining because each dungeon have their own theme, their own thoughts, and cool perspective. But man, the combat as a whole was exhausting.

The combat in the game doesn't click with me. None of the combat music hit me. At first I love the battle theme, but hearing it over and over is boring. I even had to mute it and listen to Persona 3 and Persona 5 combat instead. I'm totally aware and prepared on such manner, but with no shifting, theurgy, changing team order, element effects, and boring bosses phase, it's really boring. I even almost freaked out when they're almost going for Tartarus approach again, but i'm glad there's less floor and more sophisticated design instead. 

I really enjoyed this one. I really love the colorful theme, wholesome friendship, and fun premise. The main character is really cool too, always trying to aura farm every moment. Thankfully his hands are not in the pocket all the time. Persona 4 Golden fulfilled my expectation as a good sequel and as a whole Persona game. The only thing that it can't be better than the score I give is that the combat is becoming more and more bland as I go through the older game. Outside of reviews, It might be my last Persona game to experience too after Persona 3 Reload and Persona 5 Royal because I think... I get it. I give Persona 4 Golden 8,5/10.

An amazing story, great characters, a fantastic world and soundtrack. This is arguably the best entry in the series even still.

The only part that hasn't aged the best is the combat and journeying throughout the procederally generated dungeons. This can drag for quite a bit by the end.

Stick past that and you will not be disappointed in P4G. Looking forward to the remake and returning to Inaba.

You play as a high school student, suddenly forced into living with relatives in the middle of nowhere. Your uncle is a hard-ass that's uncomfortable around the kid he already has. Your cousin is a small, high-pitched—and slightly endearing—child. And the first "friends" (you don't really get to choose them) you make are an immature manager's son, and a girl who's trying too hard to be Bruce Lee.

Yet, you somehow end up liking them. Unironically. Your uncle is struggling to fill the hole his deceased wife left behind, your cousin is trying to bridge the gap between her father and her, the immature classmate is grieving someone who's death he blames himself over, and the Bruce Lee girl tries to protect others from bullying. 

These four only represent the first of many great characters you encounter. From the younger brother of a murder victim, to a step-mother trying to connect with her husband's child, to the adopted son of a rich family discovering his own sense of self and learning about where he came from. You'll inevitably spend dozens of hours exploring several of these amazing characters, to the point that it actually overshadows the murders you're trying to solve.

Oh yeah, this game's actually a supernatural murder mystery JRPG. It's actually really good at being that as well. You end up spending most of your hours on the game's social features because they're just that good. There isn't a lot to say about the murder mystery itself because... it's just best experienced blind, and I mean completely blind to any spoilers. They somehow made the murderer into my favourite character in the game, which is saying a lot given the strong competition.

If you've scrolled down far enough to see this review, you're clearly interested in buying the game. Maybe someone recommended it to you because of the characters, or the mystery. Maybe you've heard good things about the game's battle system. Maybe you've just heard one of the many bangers included in the game's soundtrack. Regardless of why, I have one last thing to say:

This is genuinely one of the greatest JRPGs ever made, it has some of the best characters ever written, and it has arguably the best video game soundtrack ever made. It's truly close to being a 'perfect' all-round game, and the flaws it does possess feel pale compared to the many strengths Persona 4 Golden has. If you're thinking about buying, just do it. Then you can spend the next 20 years aggressively promoting it to every gamer you know, like the rest of us do.
